comment: Premises met, price above EMAs, bullish structure. Ratio was good, missed entry by very little, needs refinement.

In an uptrend, EMAs respected, unfilled bullish gap that retail traders would try to close - good scenario for the market to trap them. Entered on indicator + trader vision, acceptable ratio. Could have exited earlier given reversal risk.
Same rationale as buena entrada ref_01.
Same rationale as buena entrada ref_01.
Similar to op_1 but does not break the double-bottom (W) neckline. Meets the EMA rule but slightly rushed.
Overall sideways market, a bearish trendline breaks and liquidity is swept; entered on structure and price being above/'fighting' the EMAs.

Last days were sideways, could be read as an attempt to continue the uptrend. Personally consider this entry rushed - tried to get ahead of the EMAs. Could be risky. Won despite the rule break, treat as luck, not validation.
Manipulation pattern seen before, EMAs well placed - good trade for me, market had already eaten the liquidity below.

That kind of slow-climbing structure can go either way; shorted because macro context was bearish and price was below the EMAs. Note: trades closer to EMA200 tend to be the most successful - that contact increases setup potential.

operation_name: Prefiero no hablar lo muestro por honestidad
comment: First trade was revenge for the previous loss (op #9). Chaotic chart, no clear dominant structure. Real loss on paper trading, logged for honesty.

Don't know why, wanted to trade and didn't respect my own rules. EMAs were set up for a short and, in hindsight, so was the structure. Annotated directly on the chart: 'porque no sigues el plan!!!' (why don't you follow the plan!!!).

Trend considered bearish, price repeatedly fights EMA200 and fails to hold above it. Even with a long signal, EMA reading is prioritized - the indicator is only a guide. Clean demonstration of EMA200 power.
Everything seemed to line up; a large green candle was a stop-hunt/news spike. Direction read was correct - EMA200 context still valid - but the stop was hit by the wick before the move completed.
Stopped out by a wick, a justified/valid setup - could refine entry type to adjust SL/TP, but this was a rigorous, valid trade whose stop was simply too tight for the sweep.

operation_name: no se dio era buena idea zona clave
comment: Entry did not trigger, was a good setup - price was above EMA200 so entry was a conditional (limit) order if price dropped, but it never triggered. Same idea played out days later in trade #16.
Since op #15 never triggered, looked for the entry via structure instead - same underlying rationale/setup.

Two trades shown in the same chart, same underlying premise: left one more conservative, right one closer to my actual trading style. Premise: price briefly breaks above EMA200 then falls. Right-side trade has more confluence/safety points and is the one counted for data.
